description_long: This house features a simple rectangular facade constructed from gray masonry, with a flat dark roofline and minimal decorative elements. The front is defined by a single arched wooden door with a small round peephole and a matching arched window with divided panes, both framed by subtle molding. The walls are punctuated by small, evenly spaced brick details, adding a touch of texture to the otherwise smooth surface. The structure appears to be a single-story building, with no visible upper levels or roof overhangs. The rear of the house is plain, featuring only a large, arched door and no windows. The overall design is utilitarian, with a slight nod to Gothic or medieval influences in the shape of the door and window arches.
description: A single-story gray masonry house with a flat roof, arched wooden door, and matching arched window, featuring minimal decorative details and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched wooden door with peephole and a matching arched window, both set in a gray masonry wall with subtle brick accents.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the extreme thinness of the structure, with no visible windows or doors on this edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a large arched door centered on a plain gray wall, with no windows or additional features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile of the house with no openings or details.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched wooden door with a small round peephole, no steps or porch, framed by subtle molding.
